text.title: e2e-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Seven antipatterns ranked by frequency, each with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.description: page.waitForTimeout(5000) or any hard-coded sleep instead of element/navigation waits.
antipattern.symptom: Test passes locally, flakes 1-5% in CI. Trace shows the element appearing after the sleep finishes.
antipattern.root-cause: Arbitrary sleeps are too short on slow CI runners, too long locally — they never match real readiness state.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `await expect(locator).toBeVisible()` or `await page.waitForURL(/dashboard/)`. Playwright auto-waits for actionability.

antipattern.description: CSS selectors or dynamic IDs (#submit-btn-1234) as locators.
antipattern.symptom: Suite breaks en masse after a style refactor or framework hash-rename. Tests show "Locator not found" but element is on the page.
antipattern.root-cause: CSS selectors couple tests to implementation details that change for non-functional reasons. Dynamic IDs change on every render.
antipattern.fix: Add stable `data-testid` on the element; use `page.getByTestId('submit')` or `page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' })`.

antipattern.description: Tests sharing a single user account across parallel workers.
antipattern.symptom: Tests pass individually, fail in `--workers=4` mode. Errors like "user logged out" or "stale session" appear mid-test.
antipattern.root-cause: Concurrent password changes, profile updates, or session mutations from parallel tests collide on shared state.
antipattern.fix: Use per-worker accounts: `createTestUser({ suffix: workerInfo.workerIndex })` or per-test users with timestamp suffix.

antipattern.description: Visual snapshots committed from a macOS runner, diffed against a Linux CI runner.
antipattern.symptom: Pixel-diff failures on text rendering and shadow anti-aliasing; failures cluster on the @visual tag.
antipattern.root-cause: Anti-aliasing and font rendering differ across OS; even subpixel hinting changes between Linux distros.
antipattern.fix: Capture and update visual baselines only on the Linux CI runner: `playwright test --update-snapshots` in a Linux-only workflow job.

antipattern.description: page.locator(...).click({ force: true }) to bypass actionability checks.
antipattern.symptom: Click reports success but the resulting assertion fails because the click hit a covering overlay or a disabled button.
antipattern.root-cause: `force: true` bypasses Playwright's visibility, enabled, and interactability checks — it hides real UI bugs.
antipattern.fix: Remove `{ force: true }`. If the element is genuinely obscured, fix the UI (close modal, scroll into view) or use `locator.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded()`.

antipattern.description: E2E suite growing beyond 200 tests with no nightly/critical distinction.
antipattern.symptom: CI run time exceeds 30 minutes; developers start running `--grep` subsets locally; suite gets disabled on busy days.
antipattern.root-cause: Logic branches got promoted into E2E instead of unit/integration; no enforced cap.
antipattern.fix: Tag tests `@smoke`/`@critical`/`@nightly`. Pre-deploy runs only `@smoke`. Demote pure-logic tests to unit/integration layer.

antipattern.description: Trace ZIP files committed to git or stored in public CI artifacts without scrubbing.
antipattern.symptom: Auth tokens visible in trace viewer; secrets-scanner alerts on `.zip` artifacts.
antipattern.root-cause: Playwright traces capture full network HAR including Authorization headers and Set-Cookie.
antipattern.fix: Set `trace: 'on-first-retry'` (not `'on'`); add `test-results/**/trace.zip` to `.gitignore`; use HAR filters in `playwright.config.ts`.
